title: The Sequel Theory Thread
wikitext:
[]: The "Sequel Theory" Thread (Now With Spoilers) Post by V » Thu Nov 29, 2007 11:32 pm Behold! The "Sequel Theory" thread everyone wanted but never created for some reason. If you are unfamiliar with the "sequel theory" stuff, it's a suggestion that originated with the first audience impressions of the movie and refuses to go away. Kind of like fem-Kaworu, but even more annoying. Try to keep the "sequel theory" banter here. Please. Thanks. This starts off with a messy split from the "Moon Giant" thread (and includes various other splits). Ornette's post couldn't be split out, so copy-pasting it into V's. -Reichu ________________________________________ Ornette Thu Nov 29, 2007 11:07 am Phantom_Blitz wrote: (Considering REbuild is a sequel and not a remake)

[]: Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Nov 29, 2007 11:50 pm NO, CAUSE THEY WOUDLN'T HAVE MADE SUCH A BIG DEAL ABOUT NOT BEING ABLE TO RECOVER THE SPEAR happy NAveryW?

[]: Post by NAveryW » Fri Nov 30, 2007 12:37 am The bold answer doesn't go with the bold question, you know...

[]: Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Nov 30, 2007 1:26 am I'll edit it just for you.

[]: Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Nov 30, 2007 5:51 am This is more like the Halo speculation at the Halo Story Page than anything else: wild, loony and utterly lacking in meaning. This is all pointless. Mass Production Evangelions, conspiracy, MPEs, conspiracy... I'm taking the "Rebuild picks off from where old Evangelion ended, and the whole thing was reset to when Shinji came to Tokyo-3" with a huge grain of salt.

[]: Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Nov 30, 2007 6:33 am I wouldn't put it past Anno to do it but I'm not jumping on that bandwagon just yet.

[]: Post by Rave_Epic » Fri Nov 30, 2007 6:41 pm It better not be a sequel.

[]: Post by chee » Fri Nov 30, 2007 7:08 pm Anything is possible. The Moon Giant could be the emaciated corpse of the Stay-Puft Marshmallow Man, it could be the love-child of Andy Dick and Eartha Kitt, it would Steve Job's next step in the "iDomination" of Earth, or it could Nuck Chorris in embryonic form. Rebuild could be a sequel, it could all be Shinji's delusion as he's on the beach at the end of EoE, it could be an entirely new work, it could be the a computer simulation implanted into Bob Saget's head to keep him from realizing his destructive power, or it could be a really long Pizza Hut commerical a la Darker than BLACK .

[]: Post by marmotgirl » Fri Nov 30, 2007 8:48 pm chee wrote: or it could Chuck Norris in embryonic form. chuck norris in embryonic form chuck norris

[]: Post by Ornette » Sat Dec 01, 2007 5:30 am chee wrote: Anything is possible. The Moon Giant could be the emaciated corpse of the Stay-Puft Marshmallow Man, it could be the love-child of Andy Dick and Eartha Kitt, it would Steve Job's next step in the "iDomination" of Earth, or it could Nuck Chorris in embryonic form. Rebuild could be a sequel, it could all be Shinji's delusion as he's on the beach at the end of EoE, it could be an entirely new work, it could be the a computer simulation implanted into Bob Saget's head to keep him from realizing his destructive power, or it could be a really long Pizza Hut commerical a la Darker than BLACK .

[]: Post by cat42 » Sat Dec 01, 2007 11:57 am Re build is a sequel. Here's what happened: Yui somehow, over several thousand years of floating all alone in outer space, somehow obtained god powers. She decided she would would go back in time, but lacked the ability to do that, so she accidently recreated the earth and everything exactly as she remembered it. However, since it's been several thousand years, her memory has gotten a bit muddled. THis is all just a delusion made real by the powers of a god that didn't truly fully realize she had them.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Dec 01, 2007 1:25 pm So now Yui's delusional? Personally, I thought it was obvious she obtained God-like powers once she used the Spear to set things right.... If Rebuild IS indeed a sequel to the series/EoE, I'm putting my money on Lilith somehow having a hand on it, or one of those "Rebuild's just a dream" endings. (Would be lame in a sense, but I wouldn't put it past Anno to give it a classic mindfuck trait.) As long as it's not a carbon-copy of Re-Take. I like that, but please no (Doujinshi should stay as doujinshi). -_-

[]: Post by V » Sat Dec 01, 2007 3:14 pm what if Rebuild, in its entirety, is just another Instrumentality or pre-3I fever-dream?

[]: Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 02, 2007 1:04 pm Reminder: Every time the "Neverending Story" hypothesis (or something even remotely similar) is mentioned, the Eva 1.0 DVD release is delayed. (Once 1.0 is out, replace <1.0> with subsequent installment's number.)

[]: Post by chee » Sun Dec 02, 2007 1:10 pm @Ornette: Of course most of those aren't probable. My point is that we have so little information that anything we come up with is mere speculation with barely anything to back it up. So here is my own futile, groundless speculation: Rebuild is a sequel, and the explanation as to how it is a sequel will most likely involve something we won't see coming at all. Or, it will somehow involve Shinji. Shinji = Anno, afterall.

[]: Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 02, 2007 7:29 pm chee wrote: Rebuild is a sequel, and the explanation as to how it is a sequel will most likely involve something we won't see coming at all. Or, it will somehow involve Shinji. Shinji = Anno, afterall.

[]: Post by Shiro » Sun Dec 02, 2007 7:37 pm I don't like Neverending Story at all, but I do think that Rebuild is a sequel. There's too many things there to make it a retelling. Kaworu on the ****ing moon and his dialogue, the moon having GNR's bloodstain, red oceans, stuff like that.

[]: Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 02, 2007 8:07 pm I'm using "Neverending Story" as a sardonic way of referring to the idea that Webuild somehow occurs in the same continuity as the original. (I forget how the eponymous book used the phrase, though. Haven't read it in ages.) So, remember. Every time you help propagate this idea before we really know anything, you are committing a grave sin and you should be ashamed of yourself. If it ends up being true, you can laugh at me later. But this is rumor control -- remember the facts.

[]: Post by Shiro » Sun Dec 02, 2007 8:32 pm OK, I understand. BUT: I'd be interested to hear your reasoning on why the seas are red and why there's a bloodstain on the moon, as well as Kaworu-kun being on the moon, and all the meaningless aesthetic changes like Lilith's mask.

[]: Post by ThroneofDravaris » Sun Dec 02, 2007 8:41 pm It looks cool.
